crashes.signature: EMPTY: no frame data available; EmptyMinidump

bug_analysis.root_cause: Insufficient error handling in DCSurfaceVideo::CallVideoProcessorBlt() method related to the Super Resolution setting
symptoms: Crashes in JavaScript garbage collection during allocation
reproduction_steps: Trigger video processing that calls DCSurfaceVideo::CallVideoProcessorBlt()
bug_analysis.technical_details: The function DCSurfaceVideo::CallVideoProcessorBlt() lacked proper error handling which led to crashes often related to memory allocation failures and garbage collection during video processing with Super Resolution enabled.
bug_analysis.user_impact: Users experienced crashes when using the Super Resolution feature, potentially causing browser instability and data loss.

common_patterns: Crashes related to memory allocation failures during garbage collection
Out Of Memory (OOM) conditions triggering crash in the JS engine
Crashes occurring during nursery eviction or chunk allocation
potential_root_causes: Insufficient memory handling or error propagation during GC nursery eviction
Lack of robust error handling for allocation failures in Super Resolution setting or related subsystems
relevant_fix_clues: Bug 1883577 aims to add error handling to the Super Resolution setting
Similar crash is described as 'Failed to allocate new chunk during GC', indicating the crash is triggered by unhandled OOM during GC
No resolved status or fix details found for the similar bugs, implying the root cause remains unaddressed
similar_bugs_context.summary: These similar bugs suggest that crashes are caused by unhandled out-of-memory allocation failures during garbage collection, particularly when evicting the nursery or allocating new chunks. The lack of robust error handling for these OOM conditions, especially in relation to the Super Resolution setting, appears to be a common root cause. Adding error handling for memory allocation failures during GC and related settings is likely needed to prevent these crashes.

similar_bugs_analysis.summary: These similar bugs suggest that crashes are caused by unhandled out-of-memory allocation failures during garbage collection, particularly when evicting the nursery or allocating new chunks. The lack of robust error handling for these OOM conditions, especially in relation to the Super Resolution setting, appears to be a common root cause. Adding error handling for memory allocation failures during GC and related settings is likely needed to prevent these crashes.

refined_analysis.root_cause: Lack of error handling for memory allocation failures in gfx/layers/DCSurfaceVideo.cpp's DCSurfaceVideo::CallVideoProcessorBlt() when Super Resolution is enabled.
symptoms: Crashes triggered by out-of-memory (OOM) conditions during video processing
JavaScript garbage collection crashes related to allocation failures
reproduction_steps: Trigger video processing that invokes DCSurfaceVideo::CallVideoProcessorBlt()
refined_analysis.technical_details: DCSurfaceVideo::CallVideoProcessorBlt() performs memory allocations without checking for failures. Under memory pressure, especially with Super Resolution active, this leads to unhandled exceptions and crashes during video processing and JS garbage collection.
refined_analysis.user_impact: Crashes reduce browser stability and risk user data loss when using Super Resolution.
proposed_fix_approach: Add checks after each memory allocation in DCSurfaceVideo::CallVideoProcessorBlt() to detect failure.
Propagate errors safely to prevent unhandled exceptions.
Implement fallback or fail-safe logic to avoid crashing during video processing and JS garbage collection.
refined_analysis.optimized_context: Crashes occur in gfx/layers/DCSurfaceVideo.cpp's DCSurfaceVideo::CallVideoProcessorBlt() due to missing error handling for memory allocation failures when Super Resolution is enabled. Adding allocation failure checks and robust error handling in this function will prevent OOM-induced crashes during video processing and JavaScript garbage collection.
refined_analysis.refinement_critique: The analysis identifies the root cause, symptoms, and affected components well but is overly verbose and somewhat repetitive. It lacks a concise, focused summary and an explicit stepwise fix plan. Although it mentions the exact source file (gfx/layers/DCSurfaceVideo.cpp) and function (DCSurfaceVideo::CallVideoProcessorBlt()), it does so inconsistently. The reproduction steps are minimal but adequate. The proposed fix approach is described at a high level but not broken down into actionable steps. The analysis is mostly self-contained but could benefit from clearer, compact phrasing and stronger emphasis on the key file and function to guide AI-driven fixes better.

filtered_context.concise_summary: gfx/layers/DCSurfaceVideo.cpp's DCSurfaceVideo::CallVideoProcessorBlt() lacks error handling for memory allocation failures when Super Resolution is enabled, causing OOM-induced crashes during video processing and JS garbage collection. Add allocation failure checks, propagate errors safely, and implement fallback logic to prevent crashes.
core_symptoms: Crashes triggered by out-of-memory conditions during video processing
JavaScript garbage collection crashes related to allocation failures
technical_constraints: Check each memory allocation in CallVideoProcessorBlt() for failure
Propagate errors to avoid unhandled exceptions
Implement fail-safe fallback to maintain stability under memory pressure
